title: Estimate latent tyre condition with a simple state-space residual model and feed its state/uncertainty as features
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation research models lap time as observed output from a latent degradation state with pit stops acting as resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation research models lap time as observed output from a latent degradation state with pit stops acting as resets. Your current features use raw degradation and rolling lap times, but not an inferred hidden tyre-health state or uncertainty, which may be closer to the real pit decision variable.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight per-compound or per-race recursive filter on historical laps to estimate latent pace loss and degradation slope from `LapTime`, `LapTime_Delta`, `TyreLife`, and `Cumulative_Degradation`, resetting on `PitStop=1`. Export features like filtered degradation level, one-step predicted pace loss, slope acceleration, and posterior uncertainty.
expected_effect: Could separate noisy lap-time spikes from true tyre fade and sharpen decision boundaries near undercut/overcut windows.
risk: A poorly specified filter can just restate existing rolling means and add noise; careful leakage control is required so all state estimates are strictly historical.
